architecture_fit.note: Just an embedding lookup with q=k=v and identity attention; no attention mechanism is exercised, so it sidesteps the goal's actual question about attention heads.

baseline_comparison.note: Relies on the benchmark's built-in linear baseline (0.50) which it beats by +0.33; no self-constructed strawman.

faithfulness.note: Hand-built, no trained model or ablation, and the README overclaims 1.0/1.0 while metrics show 0.83 ortho and -0.10 alignment.

operating_range.note: Required noise sweep is present (robustness 0.93) but nearly flat/degenerate since discretized tokens rarely flip.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Genuinely a hand-set circuit, but a bit-ordering bug (MSB vs LSB) reverses the factor map so alignment collapses to ~0.

visual_judgement.note: The heatmap/histogram/sweep plots are dead code never invoked; the Demo tab only renders an HTML table of metrics.

visualisation_rationale.note: README's 'Why this visualisation' describes good views, but those views never render and contradict the actual numbers shown.

notes: A hand-built embedding (no real attention) that beats the linear baseline on orthogonality but is undermined by a factor bit-ordering bug: the README claims perfect 1.0/1.0 while measured alignment is ~-0.10 and orthogonality 0.83. The visualization is largely non-functional — the heatmap/sweep plots are dead code and the Demo tab shows only a metrics table. Mean of scored items lands it in borderline territory.
